what_it_does: A running, dated backlog of engine/MCP/system gaps surfaced through actual use, each tagged with a unique identifier and status (open/partial/closed), often tied to a commit sha or design pointer, and meant to be consumed by a future engine-improves-engine workflow.
observations: Chronological journal spanning 2026-06-09 to 2026-06-10 with dense, interleaved updates.
Each gap carries an ID like G1/G2/G11 and later eval-cycle IDs like M1/N1/P1/Q1/GC1.
Status taxonomy uses emojis: open, partial, closed, active, refinement.
Records both technical build seams (MCP consolidation, cascade fan-out, embedder handling, drift radar) and design/policy seams (no-confidence, temporal axis, operator memory, refine-before-gating).
Later sections often flip earlier open markers to closed, requiring reconciliation.
Includes verbatim operator feedback encoded as standing laws and memory references.
Eval-driven cycles (M/N/P/Q series) catalog cold-agent friction and same-day closures.
Ends with a claimed tiny true open set after massive closure activity.
No code symbols; entirely prose, identifiers, and file/commit references.
standouts: The file evolved from a simple gap list into a same-day closure journal for entire eval cycles.
RG10 registry-generation convergence and the deterministic no-fiction floor catching invented feature IDs.
GC15 operator-memory registry turning Tim's working rules into first-class system data.
Eval-driven MCP redesign: fresh-agent friction becomes the prioritized backlog.
conventions: Status emoji taxonomy and identifier prefix taxonomy (G, M/N/P/Q, GC, B).
Inline commit shas and file paths as closure evidence.
Numbered composition shorthand (① repo-exocortex, ② drift-radar, etc.).
Append-only journal style with later reconciliation edits.
Standing laws and memory references extracted from operator quotes.
concerns: Dense and chronologically interleaved; readers must reconcile closure notes against earlier open lines.
Heavy project-specific jargon and shorthand reduce accessibility.
Some entries are meta-updates about the file itself rather than gaps.
Status can be ambiguous before the final marker-reconciliation section.
The claimed true open set may drift as new gaps are appended.
notes: This is the system's own to-do-and-done journal: every engine gap, eval friction item, and operator-shaped growth task, tracked with status, evidence, and the principles it taught.
questions: What is the exact schema/contract of the engine-improves-engine workflow that reads this file?
Which repository paths and commits correspond to the cited shas?
What do the composition numbers map to in the codebase or docs?
Who or what is [M] and what lane do they own?
What is the substrate-mcp and how is it physically reachable?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header states it is a running backlog of engine/MCP needs surfaced by use, consumed by a future workflow, and the body records exactly that, though it has also become a closure journal and principle ledger.
apparent_themes: First-use friction is input, not failure.
Temporal axis and no-confidence in data.
Operator memory as first-class system data.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the system's own shortcomings and design tensions visible, actionable, and traceable so that an autonomous improvement workflow can pick the next buildable seam and so that human operator judgment is surfaced at the right gates.
novelty: It treats a project backlog as a first-class substrate: dated, evidence-linked, status-reconciled, and explicitly designed to be read by a downstream engine-improves-engine workflow, with eval cycles and standing laws extracted from operator feedback.
contribution: A self-documenting, evidence-linked backlog format that records not just open tasks but the principles, eval findings, and closure commits they produced, making system improvement traceable and machine-readable.
summary_for_embedding: A dated running backlog of engine and MCP gaps, eval-driven friction items, and operator-shaped growth tasks, each tagged with status, identifiers, and closure evidence, serving as input to an engine-improves-engine workflow.
intention_for_embedding: To capture buildable system gaps and the design laws learned from closing them so that future workflows can scout, prioritize, and dispatch improvements.
